title: marina holwayi
intro: Marina holwayi, commonly known as the Mexican evening primrose, is a type of flowering plant native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide the health benefits. The therapeutic actions of Marina holwayi include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, promote wound healing, and exhibit antiseptic properties. Bioactive constituents of Marina holwayi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
intro_study: According to "International journal of clinical pharmacology research", marina holwayi (ginseng extract) was found to have positive effects on health-related quality of life in menopausal women, particularly in depression, well-being, and health subscales.
side_effects: Marina holwayi can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems in some individuals. Ingesting the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's essential oils may cause headaches, dizziness, and fatigue in people with sensitive skin or respiratory systems. Improper use of the Marina holwayi plant can also exacerbate existing health conditions, such as asthma or eczema.
